item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want a sequel to this where dado makes something that cures cancer, but also kills every rabbit within a half-mile radius.

Edit- Also, dado is damn near omnipotent and he considers curing cancer very tricky? This opens up some fascinating speculation. Is cancer itself an anomalous disease?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Duress: Threats, violence, constraints, or other action used to coerce someone into doing something against their will or better judgement.

Did you mean "distress"? ;)

Also I'm sort of getting the feeling here that dado is neither human nor a native of Earth… so whilst his direct understanding of English is reasonably good, a lot of the semantics and context are lost on him. Such as the idea of humans having hair and it being something that falls out during chemotherapy, and that it's not the same as the animal with a homophonic name.

The bigger question is how he manages to infiltrate his wares into the Amazon Prime warehouses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's some amazing innuendo waiting to be discovered as the answer to this, but I'm afraid I can't think of it right now

item.title: Re: Novote for Dado
item.content:encoded: That was maybe a lucky escape, though…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what if it's swallowed

item.title: Re: Yay, you made the dado
item.content:encoded: dado is the real genius here. We are all at his mercy.

item.title: Yay, you made the dado
item.content:encoded:
This is honestly a pretty happy skip, regardless of Adult Scalp Mutilation. (ASM). dado immediately offered to cure the cancer and completely understood the problem. in contrast to him messing up plutonium with potassium.
Wait. Headcanon time.
dado Purposely messes up orders that are frivolous or nefarious. (Assassination, Scams, Drugs, etc.) This is kind of messed up by Superlax, but that at least doesn't harm the patent at all. dado just hides his drug vigilantism behind a facade of misunderstanding. And this isn't messed up by the Adult Hare thing. The kid had cancer, so the drug was needed quick, so he couldn't waste time on something the drug wasn't supposed to be used for.
dado is a fucking boss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think stating it was necessary in this case, because the original intention was to get a "Hair Growth Tonic," he actually made a "hare growth tonic," and also the hares have anomalously rapid hair growth. Did dado mean for the hares to grow hair?

Considering that it seems that dado can 100% hit his intended goals (the tonic never hurts children), but is also careless in other situations, trying to figure out which effects are intentional or not might have merit in testing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a fair cop; this was my first time writing an article using someone else's character, so I wanted to stick with the formula structure-wise; my hope was that the new characterization of dado(tries to help kids rather than just working with criminals) and the wordplay/gags were enough to make a solid story.

Thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unlike a lot of people apparently, I've got a lot of room for more dado articles in my heart and while I am worried that people will just drive him into the ground (something which I have labelled "dickbottling"), I don't think we're there yet.

For me though, this just feels a little formulaic. It's like you've got a list of checkboxes for a dado article and had to make sure all of them were ticked off. It's not that it's bad in any way, it's just that it doesn't do anything different. No vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, genital lice cured as well? Or not close enough?

in 3-4 days makes me feel pretty happy about “doesn’t kill kids” dado, so yay!

I’m also extremly pleased that everybody has the same reaction to “dado offering to buckle up his spats and try to cure cancer” that I did when I thought of it.

item.title: Re: Novote for Dado
item.content:encoded: He considered fixing cancer and understood the concept of fixing cancer, and the parent just dismissed the offer. Oh lord.

item.title: Re: Novote for Dado
item.content:encoded:
The major response is “dado joke is one note,” but if an interesting story or addition to the character can be added, that means there is still room for the concept to grow. I’m proud of this one because it’s not “dado helps criminals do crime to hilarious effect,” but “dado helps kid, just not the way the dad wanted.”

Meanwhile, the first one is mainly to keep the image of giant bunnies bursting forth and taking body parts with them; I’m certainly open to a better phrasing.

Second one: that’s meant to imply that the giant bunnies are snuggling a bloody heap of a dead person. That’s going to make the bunnies messy.

Third one: fuck. Gonna fix that.

item.title: Re: Novote for Dado
item.content:encoded:
I agree. I said something to this effect on another dado SCP, but while the concept of dado was initially executed amusingly enough, the repeated application of this formula (bad English + pun + drug) resembles that of a mediocre sitcom character, as each entry rarely says anything new.

I also found some of the writing strangely phrased and stilted, though it may be down to taste.

This separation completely removed the crown of the head as well as other separation points of the subject.

I understand what this means but 'removing other separation points' is an odd thing to say.

The SCP-3802-B instances are similarly bonded with their host; this is despite the fact that their host died during separation and without regard to their own cleanliness.

I also understand this but it reads a bit like 'the host rudely went and died without considering being clean'.

far beyond that their of non-anomalous counterparts

I assume this is just an error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Plus one for me. Kind of agree that there's potential dado might be done to death soon (as much as I like it in general), but I like the more empathetic side to him/her this article showed.

(also first time trying to post in discussion section, hopefully this doesn't do something unrelated / delete website accidentally)

item.title: Re: Novote for Dado
item.content:encoded: I’m sorry you didn’t fully enjoy the skip, glad that the part about him considering curing cancer tickled you because it did me as well, and mostly curious about what grammatical errors you’re seeing. Let me know?

item.title: Novote for Dado
item.content:encoded:
This is mediocre. It's just another misinterpretation/punny take of a request. The description featured some weird phrasing and grammatical errors, and the parent's dialogue feels plastic. I think we've reached critical dado mass.

However, one part did jump out at me and stick in my brain – Dado considered fixing cancer. That was a fun thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the dado levels are off the charts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nthing the sentiment that, while this entry is fine and fun, we're pretty much inches away from maximum overdado at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no vote, because dado relations aside the effect is decent and i like the story behind it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stand by my assessment of Dado as a pharmacologic Mad Titan; here, though, he really is doing the right thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-_-
I mean, he's updated his rules for dado.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man… he keeps trying, but just can't get it right. The author did it right, though.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dado articles are like flipping a coin. Some are just the standard “oh ho ho dado don’t know English too good” but then there’s these ones that still have the misunderstanding but have a bit more behind it. Dado just genuinely wanted to make these kids happy again, even if he misunderstood how to do it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have to agree with rubberchickenzilla, this is finally feeling like one dado too far, but the story is good enough I don't feel the need to downvote.

It is unknown if this effect was intentional.

That said, is it really necessary to state this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sick of Dado spam at this point, but unlike the last few I quite liked this one. The misunderstanding was at least somewhat understandable while still being entertaining, and the wordplay was on point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this comment more than the article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Capitalize dado's name one more time I swear to a fucking wrathful god.

I'm adding that in place of the floating sixth rule, which will now be the floating seventh rule. Just as a reminder to anybody who happens by here and doesn't want to earn my ire, here are rules:

Rules 4 dado

Rule #1) dado is incredibly good at what he does.
Rule #2) dado is profoundly bad at communicating effectively.
Rule #3) u trust dado
Rule #4) dado has Amazon Prime.
Rule #5) Like all great capitalists throughout history, dado is the proud owner of laundry and tan (and potentially other businesses, dado is not picky)
NEW RULE #6) dado is dado.
Floating Rule #7) The nature of dado, being a personality of infinitely complex intrigue, cannot be defined in any single or concrete way. As such, the floating seventh rule dictates that anything sufficiently funny or narratively interesting enough can become part of the larger dado id at the whim of djkaktus (and/or others, if they prove to be sufficiently funny or narratively interesting enough).

So it is, so it shall be.

Edit: I will update this list in this discussion thread as additional rules become necessary. See the changelog below for updates.

+ Open Changelog
- Hide Changelog

Version 1.0 - Added rules (including new rule #6).
Version 1.1 - Added changelog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: kaktus actually mentioned that in chat once as an idea; it cures cancer, but also kills people who are Cancer and is lethal to crabs.

item.title: Re: Apologies for doing a double post but....
item.content:encoded:
what about people who have bald spots or have cut off hair from the sides of their head like this?

Smaller bunnies come out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with toa_vine - dado has to run out of steam eventually, but this entry was really nice. It nails what I really liked about the original dado article, which is the ambiguity whether the anomaly is a mistake/misunderstanding or just a unique approach to problem-solving (hare even better than hair, u trust dado).

Edit: also, holy shit, dado was totally prepared to take a shot at curing that kid's cancer. I would love to see an article about what would happen if he tried something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to novote this.

One the one hand I'm really getting sick of dado. dado articles are for the most part very formulaic and predictable, and this follows the formula almost to a T, including the ridiculous conversation where dado comically misunderstands something. Their weird speech/typing/whatever patterns stopped being funny to me after the first few times.

On the other hand, there's a wonderful narrative here about dado being good and wholesome and trying to help out a sick kid. I think the fact that they're not helping out criminals for once is great, and I love the idea that they genuinely made that kid's day.

I think if this didn't have the phone conversation I'd +1 it, the misunderstanding is clear enough without the pointless dadologue.

Edit: I'm sorry Kaktus, capitalisations are sorted.

item.title: Apologies for doing a double post but....
item.content:encoded: What happens to people with hair who use this? And what about people who have bald spots or have cut off hair from the sides of their head like this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this characterization of dado. He definitely seems like the kind of guy that talks fast/over people. It goes well with how he is constantly mistaking what people are actually wanting out of his medicine (he works fast and thinks fast, so he trips over small things like homophones). Specific, but still wrong, is sort of the dado trademark, imo.

+1 u trust dado

Edit: dado doing this "bone pro no" for a child is super sweet. I'd like to believe he's always like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I’m glad you enjoyed it! I didn’t want to stray too far off of formula since this is my first time writing for someone else’s PoI, but the tiny switch of him being “helpful in his own way” made me happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There have been quite a few dado articles in the last month, to the point where I was worried I'd be burned out on him.

Thank you for proving me wrong. I was concerned that the "dado misunderstands request" gimmick would wear thin, but it genuinely works here because I could believe that dado understood the request perfectly, but thought this would make the children happier. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a hare raising skip

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Westrin, Varaxous, and MaliceAforethought for pushing me to make this a little more than it was, and thanks to BlazingTrail for scoping it out near the finish.

Also thanks to djkaktus for creating dado and approving of this iteration.

All-in-all, I just had that “hare growth” thing stuck in my head after reading dado stuff and had to get it out; thankfully the top of my head and other body parts stayed attached.

A little heartwarming:
I’m really happy with my headcanon that dado tried not to hurt the kids, even if he didn’t/chose not to understand what he was actually supposed to do.

A little horrifying:
I got some WTFs in crit for the Beta manifestation, so I’m happy about that.

I hope ya’ll enjoy it.

Official Audio Adaptation Here

If you want to see more of my work, check out

TheeSherm’s Author Page & Site-42 Hub
